channel.title: Reverb — New Bass Guitars
channel.description: Recently listed bass guitars on Reverb

item.title: OLP MM - 5 String — $264.97
item.description:
$264.97 · Good · Bocchi the Shop

I bought this bass a little over a year ago as a refurbishing project. I found it on eBay in a well-loved state, no strings, a little rusty. I have been able to remove most of the rust, clean it up and give it a restring and a full setup.


A few notes:

-The fret ends are a little rough, not to the point where it causes discomfort, but I find that fine threads on a cloth get caught when wiping the strings down after a play session.

-Great natural tone, the electrics show their age a little and I'm not 100% what the knobs do exactly. But with a few adjustments you can still get a nice Musicman humbucker tone.

-I accidentally scratched the finish by the 19th fret where I removed a foam sticker. Check the pictures, it is noticeable to the player, but not out of place on an old, naturally worn bass.

-I chose the leave the thumb rest where I found it, as the screws have left holes in the pickguard and stained it somewhat. Be aware if you decide to remove it. I looked into replacing the pickguard but I don't think it's easy to find a matching shape. Better to make your own.


I think this would make a great first 5 string bass for a young player, or a rough 'throwabout' bass for a gigging player. Check the attached video for a thorough sound demo.

item.title: Schecter P-5 bass in Ivory — $999
item.description:
$999 · Brand New · LV Guitars

Schecter P-5 Electric Bass in Ivory

The Schecter P-5 delivers a comfortable, fast-playing experience built on a thin 'C' maple neck and 35" extended scale that rewards both aggressive attack and nuanced melodic work. This five-string instrument combines classic P-bass character with added depth across all strings, cutting through any mix with thick, punchy tone. The ivory finish and black hardware present understated elegance while the electronics and construction deliver the professional-grade reliability expected from a working bassist.

Playability & Construction

The bolt-on alder body with flat top pairs with a maple neck shaped to a thin 'C' profile, measuring 0.787" at the first fret and 0.866" at the twelfth for responsive, comfortable play across extended sessions. The 35" scale with 21 narrow X-jumbo frets and 16" fretboard radius on rosewood provides the extended range and articulation five-string players demand, while pearloid offset/reverse dot inlays offer clear visual reference points in lower register work.

Tone & Electronics

The Schecter USA MonsterTone-P™ bridge pickup drives authoritative low-end and midrange presence with simple volume and tone controls fitted with metal knurled knobs for reliable operation night after night. Hardware includes a Schecter high-mass bass bridge (string-through or top-load configuration), 2-way adjustable truss rod with heel access spoke wheel, and Graph Tech XL Ivory Tusq nut at 1.771" width—all contributing to solid sustain and tonal clarity across the full frequency range.

Key Features
  • Body Material: Alder with flat top contour
  • Bolt-on construction, right-handed
  • Maple neck with thin 'C' profile
  • Rosewood fretboard, 16" radius
  • 35" scale with 21 narrow X-jumbo frets
  • Pearloid offset/reverse dot inlays
  • Schecter USA MonsterTone-P™ pickup
  • Graph Tech XL Ivory Tusq nut, 1.771" width
  • Schecter lightweight tuners
  • Schecter high-mass bass bridge (string-through or top-load)
  • 2-way adjustable truss rod with heel access spoke wheel
  • Black hardware; strung with Ernie Ball 5-String Super Long (.045–.130)

This Model 2922 combines extended-range capability with the classic P-bass foundation, delivering an instrument that feels like an extension of your playing from the moment you strap it on.

item.title: Rickenbacker 4003 2004 Fireglo — $3,485.02
item.description:
$3,485.02 · Very Good · MotoMusic

2004 Rickenbacker 4003 Bass Guitar – Fireglo – Original Hard Case

An iconic bass that needs little introduction, this is a 2004 Rickenbacker 4003 finished in the timeless Fireglo sunburst. Instantly recognisable for its unmistakable looks and unmistakable sound, the 4003 has been the instrument of choice for countless legendary bass players thanks to its powerful output, incredible sustain and unique tonal character.

Presented in very good condition for its age, this 22-year-old instrument has been well cared for and remains an excellent player's example. As you would expect, there are a few small marks and minor dings consistent with careful use over the years. There is also some evidence of minor lacquer burn to the edge of the headstock caused by prolonged contact with a wall hanger. This is purely cosmetic and does not affect the playability or structural integrity of the instrument.

Our workshop has carried out a full professional inspection and setup, ensuring the bass plays beautifully with a comfortable action and is ready for its next owner.

Tonally, the 4003 delivers everything that has made the model a classic. The pair of Rickenbacker single-coil pickups produce a wonderfully articulate voice with exceptional note definition and piano-like sustain. The neck pickup offers deep, warm lows with plenty of body, while the bridge pickup adds the unmistakable bright growl and cutting midrange that allows the bass to sit perfectly in a mix. Blending the two pickups creates the famous Rickenbacker sound—rich, punchy and harmonically complex, with an aggressive edge that works equally well for classic rock, progressive music, indie, punk and modern alternative styles.

The maple body and neck-through construction contribute to the instrument's superb sustain and fast attack, while the rosewood fingerboard adds warmth and balance to the overall voice. Every note remains clear and focused, whether played with fingers or a pick.

Factory Specification

  • 2004 Rickenbacker 4003
  • Fireglo finish
  • Neck-through-body construction
  • Solid maple body with bound edges
  • Maple neck
  • Rosewood fingerboard
  • 33¼" scale length
  • 20 frets
  • Rickenbacker case supplied
  • Two Rickenbacker single-coil bass pickups
  • Dual volume and dual tone controls
  • Stereo Rick-O-Sound and standard mono outputs
  • Rickenbacker adjustable bridge and tailpiece
  • Schaller open-gear tuning machines
  • Chrome hardware

Supplied complete with its original high-quality Rickenbacker hard shell case.

A superb opportunity to own one of the most distinctive and sought-after bass guitars ever produced. With its unmistakable looks, legendary tone and classic neck-through construction, this Fireglo 4003 is equally suited to the collector, recording musician or gigging professional.

item.title: Schecter J-5 bass in Gloss Black — $1,049
item.description:
$1,049 · Brand New · LV Guitars

Schecter J-5 Electric Bass in Black

The Schecter J-5 combines a slim, comfortable neck profile with an ergonomic body contour that makes extended playing sessions feel natural. The dual MonsterTone-J pickups deliver articulate, punchy tone with impressive clarity across the frequency spectrum, capable of everything from warm vintage character to modern aggression depending on playing dynamics. This is a versatile instrument that responds authentically to both aggressive slap techniques and delicate fingerstyle work.

Playability & Construction

The thin "C"-profile maple neck with rosewood fingerboard is dimensioned at .787" at the first fret and .866" at the 12th fret, creating a fast, comfortable playing experience. The alder body with bolt-on construction and flat top contour provides excellent resonance and sustain, while the 35-inch scale length delivers tight low-end response and clear articulation across all five strings.

Tone & Electronics

The USA MonsterTone-J pickups in both neck and bridge positions offer flexible tonal control through dual volume controls and a tone knob with push-pull coil tap, allowing you to shape your sound from fat and warm to bright and aggressive. The Schecter Custom bass bridge with string-thru or top-load capability ensures stable intonation and resonant sustain, while the Graph Tech XL Ivory Tusq nut contributes clarity and harmonic definition to every note.

Key Features
  • 5-string configuration, right-handed, gloss black finish
  • Alder body with bolt-on maple neck in Thin "C" profile
  • Rosewood fingerboard with 21 narrow X-jumbo frets and offset dot inlays
  • 35-inch (889 mm) scale length with 16-inch (406 mm) fingerboard radius
  • Schecter USA MonsterTone-J pickups in neck and bridge positions
  • Dual volume controls and tone control with push-pull coil tap
  • Metal knurled knobs with set screws
  • Graph Tech XL Ivory Tusq nut, 1.771" width
  • Schecter Custom bass bridge with string-thru or top-load capability
  • Black hardware throughout
  • 2-way adjustable truss rod with heel-access spoke wheel
  • Includes Ernie Ball 5-String Super Long strings (.045–.130)

The Schecter J-5 delivers the versatility and tonal character demanded by working bassists across rock, funk, R&B, and fusion contexts.

item.title: Schecter Riot-4 Bass bass in Inferno Burst — $1,299
item.description:
$1,299 · Brand New · LV Guitars

Schecter RIOT-4 Electric Bass in Satin Inferno Burst

The RIOT-4 combines a fast, comfortable neck with a punchy, articulate tone that cuts through any mix. This 4-string bass delivers modern clarity and presence across rock, metal, and contemporary styles, while the satin Inferno Burst finish provides visual appeal without compromising playability or feel.

Playability & Build Quality

A thin C-shaped neck profile with a 34" scale ebony fingerboard and 24 narrow-jumbo frets creates a fast, responsive playing surface that accommodates both fingerstyle and slapping techniques. The 5-piece maple/walnut laminate neck is reinforced with carbon-fiber rods for stability and sustain, while the swamp ash body with poplar burl top delivers resonance and tonal complexity across the frequency spectrum.

Tone & Electronics

Dual EMG active pickups—the 35JCSX at the bridge and 35PCSX at the neck—feed into an EMG B-30 3-Band active EQ powered by an 18v supply, giving you sculpted, articulate lows and controlled highs. This electronics package produces a modern, punchy character with excellent clarity and presence in any ensemble setting.

Key Features
  • 4-string configuration, right-handed orientation
  • 5-piece maple/walnut laminate neck with carbon-fiber reinforcement
  • Swamp ash body with poplar burl top
  • Thin C-shaped neck profile
  • 34" scale ebony fingerboard with 24 narrow-jumbo frets
  • Graph Tech nut
  • EMG 35JCSX bridge pickup and EMG 35PCSX neck pickup
  • EMG B-30 3-Band active EQ with 18v power supply
  • Schecter die-cast tuning machines
  • Schecter Custom Bass String-Thru bridge
  • Black hardware throughout
  • Satin Inferno Burst finish

The RIOT-4 represents a coherent instrument design where neck feel, electronics, and construction all work in concert to deliver an inspiring playing experience.

item.title: Schecter SLS Evil Twin-5 bass in Satin Black — $1,649

The SLS ELITE-5 Evil Twin delivers refined ergonomics through its sculpted swamp ash body and ultra-access heel carve, designed to sit naturally against you during extended sessions. Neck-through construction paired with Fishman Fluence active electronics produces articulate, punchy tone with exceptional clarity across all five strings. This instrument balances aggressive attack with refined nuance, equally suited to modern rock, metal, and progressive applications as it is to funk and fusion.

Playability & Feel

The carbon fiber-reinforced 7-ply maple/walnut/padauk thru-neck delivers sustain and stability, while the thin "C" neck profile (0.787" at the 1st fret, 0.866" at the 12th fret) encourages fast, fluid movement across the fretboard. 24 X-Jumbo stainless steel frets on an ebony fingerboard with 16" radius ensure consistent playability whether executing intricate passages or laying down thunderous riffs.

Tone & Electronics

Fishman Fluence active soapbar pickups with 2-band EQ and single-coil switching via volume push-pull deliver multiple voicing options and mid-cut selections through a 3-way switch. The 35" scale length produces tight low-end definition with the clarity and responsiveness demanded by modern playing styles.

Key Features
  • Swamp ash body with neck-through construction

  • Grover 144 tuners with GraphTech XL Black Tusq nut (1.771" width)
  • Schecter Custom bridge with black hardware

The combination of neck-through sustain, premium woods, and versatile active electronics makes this bass a serious instrument for players demanding both performance and tonal flexibility.

item.title: Greco Mercury Bass Vintage MIJ Electric Bass Natural Ash 1978 w/ Hard Case — $899
item.description:
$899 · Good · Japanese Guitar Company

Up for sale is a vintage Greco Mercury Bass made in Japan.

Finished in a beautiful natural ash finish, this Mercury Bass captures the classic look and feel of late-1970s Japanese craftsmanship. These early Greco basses have developed a strong following for their solid construction, comfortable necks, and vintage-inspired tone.

This bass has recently been cleaned, restrung, and professionally set up. It plays well and is structurally sound.

Cosmetically, it shows honest play wear throughout from decades of use. Expect scattered scratches, dings, finish wear, oxidation on some of the hardware, and general cosmetic aging consistent with a vintage instrument. The wear gives it plenty of character without affecting its playability.

The neck plate is stamped L782831, dating the instrument to 1978. It features the original open-gear tuners, vintage-style bridge, rosewood fingerboard, and split-coil pickup. The ash body has particularly attractive grain that really stands out under the natural finish.

Overall, this is a great-playing vintage Japanese bass that's becoming increasingly difficult to find, especially in its original natural finish.

Please review the photos carefully, as they are part of the description.

Case: Hard

Scale Length: 34“

Nut Width: 1.59“

Fret Wear: 8/10

Action at 12F (4th): .1“

Action at 12F (1st): .07“

Weight: 9lbs 1oz

Overall Condition: 7.5/10

Cool Factor: 11 out of 10


USA Seller, check my feedback to see how I value my customers and see my other listings for more rare Japanese Guitars and other unique gear! I use recycled packaging for reduced environmental impact and ship all items 100% insured against loss or damage.


Internal Use (Bin) - PA-4-2

Playability & Build

The 21 Narrow X-Jumbo frets and 12-inch fretboard radius give you quick access across the entire range, supported by a 2-way truss rod for neck stability and adjustment.

Tone & Electronics

The dual EMG pickup configuration—EMG P5 at the neck and EMG LJ at the bridge—paired with 2-band active EMG EQ and a master volume/blend control delivers aggressive articulation and tonal flexibility. This signature model combines professional-grade construction with proven active electronics, delivering the aggressive character and tonal definition demanded by serious bass players in high-energy genres.

The bolt-on ash body paired with a 5-piece maple/jatoba neck creates a punchy, articulate tone with excellent note definition, making this instrument equally capable in rock, metal, jazz, or alternative settings. The active 3-band EQ electronics provide serious tonal flexibility, allowing you to dial in everything from warm, rounded lows to aggressive midrange bite.

Playability & Construction

Built on a 34" scale with a 40mm molded nut and 24 XJ frets, this bass maintains tight intonation and comfortable string spacing for left-handed players. The roasted jatoba fingerboard and 400mm radius combine durability with a responsive playing surface that rewards both precision technique and dynamic expression.

Tone & Electronics

The ESP-designed SB-4N neck pickup and SB-4B bridge pickup feed into a passive/active hybrid system with independent bass, mid, and treble boost/cut controls plus balance adjustment, giving you professional-grade tonal shaping without the battery drain of fully active instruments. The LTD BB-604 bridge with string-thru design and black nickel hardware ensure stable tuning and clean, defined articulation across all frequencies.

Key Features
  • Construction: Bolt-On
  • Body: Ash
  • Neck: 5-Piece Maple/Jatoba
  • Fingerboard: Roasted Jatoba

  • ESP-Designed SB-4N Neck & SB-4B Bridge Pickups
  • LTD BB-604 Bridge with String-Thru Design

This left-handed B-204SM delivers professional-quality playability, tone, and electronics in a dependable instrument built to handle the demands of serious players across any genre.

Tone & Electronics

Dual EMG 40HZ pickups powered by an 18-volt active system deliver punchy, articulate tone with impressive clarity and natural aggression. The Master Volume/Blend and 3-Band EMG Active E/Q controls provide comprehensive shaping from deep lows to crisp highs, cutting through dense mixes in studio and live settings alike.

The CV-5 delivers a comfortable, fast neck that invites exploration across melodic lines and aggressive slap passages with equal confidence. Neck thickness measures .787" at the 1st fret and .866" at the 12th fret, providing comfortable support across the full range; the Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut at 1.496" width ensures solid string definition and stable intonation.

Tone & Electronics

The EMG 35HZ bridge and neck pickups with Master Volume, Blend, and 2-Band EMG Active EQ deliver punchy, articulate tone with cutting midrange and deep low-end presence that maintains clarity at high volumes. The 9-volt clip-in battery compartment powers the active circuit consistently, while the Hipshot 'A' Style bridge with string-thru body or top-load configuration provides intonation stability and responsive sustain.

Key Features
  • Mahogany body with arched top and set-neck construction with Ultra Access
  • Creme 1-ply binding with gloss black finish
  • 3-piece maple neck with thin 'C' profile
  • 34" scale length (863mm)
  • 24 X-Jumbo frets on rosewood fretboard with 16" radius
  • Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut, 1.496" width
  • Dual EMG 35HZ humbucker pickups
  • Master Volume, Blend, and 2-Band EMG Active EQ controls
  • Hipshot 'A' Style Bass bridge with string-thru body or top-load options
  • 9-volt clip-in battery compartment
  • Black hardware throughout with metal knurled control knobs
  • Right-handed configuration, manufactured in South Korea

This Schecter Avenger combines precision engineering with the tonal authority needed for professional-level work across demanding genres.

item.title: ESP LTD B-204SM Fretless Bass in Natural Stain Finish — $649
item.description:

ESP LTD B-204SM Fretless Bass in Natural Stain Finish

The B-204SM is a nimble, expressive fretless that rewards precision and sensitivity from the player. Its extra thin U-shaped neck and roasted jatoba fingerboard invite hours of comfortable playing, while the spalted maple top adds visual appeal to an already refined instrument. This is a serious choice for players seeking articulate fretless tone without compromise.

Playability & Feel

The 5-piece maple/jatoba neck with extra thin U contour delivers minimal hand fatigue during extended sessions, and the 400mm fingerboard radius provides a balanced feel across all positions. The 40mm molded nut maintains comfortable string spacing, while the 24 fret markers on the fretless board guide intonation without the visual clutter of a fretted design.

Tone & Electronics

The passive pickup setup with ESP-designed SB-4N and SB-4B pickups captures the natural resonance of the ash body and spalted maple top, while the active 3-band EQ (volume, balance, and ABQ-3 tone control) provides sculpting power when needed. The LTD BB-604 string-through bridge with black nickel hardware maximizes sustain and clarity, essential for fretless articulation.

Key Features
  • Fretless Design with 24 fret markers for intuitive intonation reference
  • Bolt-On Construction for reliable sustain and resonance
  • 34" Scale — standard bass scale for balanced tension and traditional feel
  • Ash Body with spalted maple top for tonal clarity and visual character
  • 5-Piece Maple/Jatoba Neck — stable, durable, and responsive
  • Roasted Jatoba Fingerboard — naturally durable without finish, ideal for fretless playing
  • Extra Thin U Neck Contour for reduced hand fatigue and modern playability
  • Passive Electronics with Active 3-Band EQ — flexible tone shaping with plug-and-play simplicity
  • LTD BB-604 String-Through Bridge with black nickel hardware
  • D'Addario XL165 Strings (.045/.065/.085/.105) included

The B-204SM combines refined ergonomics, expressive electronics, and a distinctive visual aesthetic—a fretless bass built for players who understand the instrument's demands and rewards.

item.title: Fender Mustang USA 1977 - Natural Vintage Rare — $2,748.95
item.description:
$2,748.95 · Good · Daniels Gear Outlet

Zum Verkauf steht ein originaler Fender Mustang Bass aus dem Jahr 1978 in der klassischen und sehr gefragten Natural Finish Ausführung. Der Bass befindet sich im kompletten Originalzustand – es wurden keinerlei Modifikationen oder Veränderungen vorgenommen, alle Teile sind original und stammen aus der Produktionszeit. Das Baujahr lässt sich anhand der Seriennummer S708403 sowie des Neck Stamps eindeutig auf 1978 datieren.

Es handelt sich um einen typischen Mustang Bass aus der CBS-Ära mit Shortscale-Mensur (30"), der genau den charakteristischen, trockenen und mittigen Vintage-Sound liefert, für den diese Modelle bekannt sind. Der Bass sitzt hervorragend im Mix und eignet sich ideal für verschiedenste Stilrichtungen von Indie über Rock bis hin zu Soul und Studioeinsätzen.

Der Zustand ist für das Alter sehr gut und ehrlich. Technisch ist das Instrument einwandfrei und sofort spielbereit. Optisch weist der Bass leichte, altersbedingte Gebrauchsspuren auf, die absolut im Rahmen sind und den Vintage-Charakter unterstreichen. Es handelt sich ausdrücklich nicht um ein künstliches Relic, sondern um natürliche Spielspuren, die auf den Bildern transparent dokumentiert sind. Strukturelle Schäden oder Risse sind nicht vorhanden.

Gerade in dieser originalen und unverbastelten Form werden Mustang Bässe aus den späten 70ern zunehmend seltener und sind sowohl für Spieler als auch für Sammler interessant. Die Natural-Varianten mit Esche-Body haben zudem eine besonders attraktive Optik und erfreuen sich wachsender Beliebtheit.

Der Bass wird mit einem Koffer geliefert, dieser ist jedoch nicht original.

Dies ist ein Privatverkauf unter Ausschluss von Garantie, Gewährleistung und Rücknahme. Der Verkauf erfolgt nach bestem Wissen und Gewissen, alle Angaben sind ehrlich und transparent.





English:


For sale is an original Fender Mustang Bass from 1978 in the classic and highly desirable natural finish. The bass is in completely original condition with no modifications or changes – all parts are original and period-correct. The production year can be clearly verified by the serial number (S708403) and the neck stamp.

This is a typical CBS-era Mustang Bass featuring a 30" short scale, delivering the well-known punchy, dry and mid-focused vintage tone these instruments are loved for. It sits perfectly in a mix and works great across a wide range of styles, from indie and rock to soul and studio applications.

The overall condition is very good for its age and completely honest. Technically, the bass is fully functional and ready to play. Cosmetically, it shows minor signs of wear consistent with age and use, which add to its authentic vintage character. This is not a relic instrument – all wear is natural and clearly shown in the photos. There are no cracks or structural issues.

Original and unmodified Mustang Basses from the late 1970s are becoming increasingly hard to find, making this a great option for both players and collectors. The natural ash body versions are especially sought after due to their distinctive look.

The bass comes with a case, however it is not the original one.

This is a private sale with no warranty, guarantee, or returns. All information is provided to the best of my knowledge and in full transparency.

item.title: Ibanez SR1305 IPO2 Premium Soundgear 5 String Bass 2016 Natural Flat — $1,323.44
item.description:
$1,323.44 · Mint · MotoMusic

2016 Ibanez Premium SR1305-IP02 5-String Bass – Mint Condition

A superb example of the acclaimed Ibanez Premium Series, this 2016 SR1305-IP02 is presented in mint condition with only a handful of hours of studio use from new. Renowned as a true "workhorse" among professional bassists, the SR1305 combines exceptional build quality, effortless playability and an incredibly versatile tonal palette, making it equally at home in the studio, on stage or in the rehearsal room.

Built in Indonesia as part of Ibanez's Premium range, the SR1305 features a beautifully figured Ash/Panga Panga top over an African Mahogany body, delivering a balanced, articulate voice with plenty of punch and sustain. The slim 5-piece Atlas-5 neck, constructed from Panga Panga and Purpleheart, is paired with a Panga Panga fingerboard with premium fret edge treatment, providing the fast, comfortable feel that the SR series is famous for.

Electronics consist of a pair of Nordstrand™ Big Single pickups, paired with the highly versatile Ibanez Custom Electronics 3-band EQ featuring a bypass switch and passive tone control. Whether you're after modern hi-fi clarity, vintage warmth or aggressive punch, this bass has an impressive range of usable tones at your fingertips.

Hardware includes the highly regarded Mono-Rail V bridge, allowing each string to vibrate independently for outstanding sustain, clarity and precise intonation.

This particular bass has received a full workshop inspection in our service department and is ready for its next owner.

Factory Specification

  • 2016 Ibanez Premium SR1305-IP02
  • Premium Series
  • 5-string electric bass
  • Ash/Panga Panga top on African Mahogany body
  • 5-piece Atlas-5 Panga Panga/Purpleheart neck
  • Panga Panga fingerboard with Premium fret edge treatment
  • 24 medium frets
  • 34" scale length
  • Nordstrand™ Big Single pickups (neck and bridge)
  • Ibanez Custom Electronics 3-band EQ
  • EQ bypass with passive tone control
  • Mono-Rail V bridge
  • Gold hardware
  • Ibanez Gig Bag included

A genuinely outstanding professional-grade instrument offering premium craftsmanship, exceptional versatility and immaculate condition throughout.

item.title: Sandberg California TM5 Superlight Dark Tobacco Aged — $2,617.57
item.description:
$2,617.57 · Brand New · Dijkmans Guitars

Sandberg California TM2 Superlight in Dark Tobacco Aged – twee pickups, één karakter

De Sandberg California TM2 Superlight in Dark Tobacco Aged is een bas die direct indruk maakt. De diepe, warme tint van de Dark Tobacco Aged afwerking geeft dit instrument een rijpe, vintage uitstraling die op elk podium de aandacht trekt. De TM2 configuratie met twee pickups biedt een extra dimensie in klankvorming, van warm en rond tot strak en gearticuleerd. De Superlight constructie houdt het gewicht laag zonder in te leveren op toon of sustain.

De Swamp Ash body draagt bij aan een open, luchtig toonkarakter met een natuurlijke attack. Het Pau Ferro fretboard speelt soepel en snel, met een heldere toonaanslag die bijzonder goed werkt voor funk, soul en rock. De 34"-mensuur geeft de bas de spanning en definitie die je van een professioneel instrument mag verwachten.

De Sandberg Blacklabel pickups leveren een veelzijdig klankpalet, en de 2-band EQ met Split Switch geeft je directe controle over je geluid. De chrome hardware en het zwarte pickguard completeren de look strak en functioneel. Sandberg bouwt elk instrument met de hand in Duitsland, en dat is aan elk detail te voelen en te horen.

De Sandberg California TM2 Superlight Dark Tobacco Aged is beschikbaar als pre-order. Kom langs in onze winkel of bel ons voor meer informatie over de Sandberg California TM2 Superlight Dark Tobacco Aged. Verwachte levertijd: Q4 2027.

Guitar will be expertly boxed and protected for safe shipping. Please see my seller evaluations for feedback on my packing skills :)

I ship worldwide by courier: UPS, DHL, TNT, Fedex, etc (depending which is cheaper on a given day). I will ship to Australia and Asia, but costs will be higher, contact me for a precise quote.

All import duties are the responsibility of the buyer.

item.title: 1962 Fender Precision Bass - Sunburst - OHSC — $22,027.15
item.description:
$22,027.15 · Excellent · Andy Baxter Bass & Guitars Ltd

1962 Fender Precision Bass in excellent condition with a Sunburst finish over an Alder body. It has a wide C profile Maple neck with a beautiful dark rosewood veneer fingerboard, a 7.25" radius and clay dots. Serial: 78558.

Superb all-original example in fantastic condition. It is supplied with its original brown tolex case ( also in excellent condition) and the original leather strap and instruction manual.

This is a fantastic playing and sounding instrument. It has an amazing neck, and the frets have plenty of life left in them, having most likely been strung with flats its whole life. There are a few small scuffs & dings and patches of general playwear to the body finish, but nothing major. Overall, it's in wonderful condition, and the Sunburst remains vibrant. The back of the neck has some playwear through the finish along the edges. The headstock is very clean with just a few small scuffs along the top edge. Everything remains structurally sound with no breaks or repairs, including the pickguard, which is fully intact with minimal shrinkage.

This bass has been fully set up in our workshop with a fresh set of La Bella 760FS 45-105 flat-wound strings. The neck is straight, and the truss rod works correctly. The neck date is Nov '62. The tone pot dates to the 37th week of '62 and the volume pot to the 23rd week of '61. The pickups have Black bobbins and original winds. Weight: 8 lbs 8 oz / 3.86 kg. Nut width: 44 mm. Depth at 1st fret: 21.5 mm.

item.title: Aria Pro2 ASB-60/IGB-60 Thru-neck PJ!! — $478.60
item.description:
$478.60 · Very Good · Napanee Vintage Guitars

This bass is pretty hard to find on N. America. It is more common in Japan. It is medium scale.

I think it is likely made in Korea. It is very well made.

It is my understanding from researching these basses that they were designed as an affordable SB.

Thought it has a PJ config., It shares a lot of the SB characteristics. It has a similar bridge, and a thru-neck. It is a great bass to play.

Also, it has 5 piece maple/walnut neck like an SB, and some form of ash/sen for the wings.

Bass functions well and is completely original. Truss Rod turns, and there is lots of fret left.

The pics should tell the story. This bass and it's full scale brother (ASB-65)are the best bang for your buck in affordable basses.  I use the ASB-65 my daily player (and I have 31 basses to choose from). Punches above it's weight for sure.
